content.problemStatement:
Firdavs is living on planet F. There are NNN cities (numbered 111 through NNN) on this planet; let's denote the value of city iii by viv_ivi‚Äč. Firdavs can travel directly from each city to any other city. When he travels directly from city xxx to city yyy, he needs to pay f(x,y)=‚ą£vy‚ąívx‚ą£+y‚ąíxf(x, y) = |v_y-v_x|+y-xf(x,y)=‚ą£vy‚Äč‚ąívx‚Äč‚ą£+y‚ąíx coins (this number can be negative, in which case he receives ‚ąíf(x,y)-f(x, y)‚ąíf(x,y) coins).

Let's define a simple path from city xxx to city yyy with length k‚Č•1k \ge 1k‚Č•1 as a sequence of cities a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aka_1, a_2, \ldots, a_ka1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,ak‚Äč such that all cities in this sequence are different, a1=xa_1 = xa1‚Äč=x and ak=ya_k = yak‚Äč=y. The cost of such a path is ‚ąĎi=1k‚ąí1f(ai,ai+1)\sum_{i=1}^{k-1} f(a_i, a_{i+1})‚ąĎi=1k‚ąí1‚Äčf(ai‚Äč,ai+1‚Äč).

You need to answer some queries for Firdavs. In each query, you are given two cities xxx and yyy, and you need to find the minimum cost of a simple path from city xxx to city yyy. Then, you need to find the length of the longest simple path from xxx to yyy with this cost.
constraints: the sum of NNN in all test cases does not exceed 5‚čÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚čÖ105
the sum of QQQ in all test cases does not exceed 5‚čÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚čÖ105
the sum of NNN in all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
the sum of QQQ in all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers v1,v2,‚Ä¶,vNv_1, v_2, \ldots, v_Nv1‚Äč,v2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,vN‚Äč.
The following QQQ lines describe queries. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers xxx and yyy.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing two space-separated integers ‚Äē the minimum cost and the maximum length.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: For the first query, there are two paths with cost 444 from city 222 to city 333:

2‚Üí1‚Üí32 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 32‚Üí1‚Üí3: cost (‚ą£4‚ąí2‚ą£+1‚ąí2)+(‚ą£5‚ąí4‚ą£+3‚ąí1)=4(|4-2|+1-2)+(|5-4|+3-1) = 4(‚ą£4‚ąí2‚ą£+1‚ąí2)+(‚ą£5‚ąí4‚ą£+3‚ąí1)=4, length 333
2‚Üí32 \rightarrow 32‚Üí3: cost ‚ą£5‚ąí2‚ą£+3‚ąí2=4|5-2|+3-2 = 4‚ą£5‚ąí2‚ą£+3‚ąí2=4, length 222

All other paths have greater costs, so the minimum cost is 444. Among these two paths, we want the one with greater length, which is 333.
